key_insight: DFT predicts that lead-free double perovskites K/Rb2SnGeX6 are stable, ductile, and strongly tunable from wide-gap to narrow-gap semiconductors by changing the halogen.
engineering_relevance: These materials are promising for environmentally safer photovoltaics, near-infrared optoelectronics, and high-temperature thermoelectric waste-heat recovery.
method: Structural stability checks using formation energy, tolerance factor, and octahedral factor
Elastic, electronic, optical, and thermoelectric property analysis across Cl/Br/I compositions
evidence_signal: The abstract reports robust cubic stability for all six compounds, direct bandgaps tunable from 1.44 eV to 0.64 eV, and a peak thermoelectric performance of ZT = 2.4 for K2SnGeI6 at 1000 K.
watch_item: The results are computational predictions, so experimental synthesis, defect tolerance, and device-level stability still need validation.
paper_title: First-Principles Study of Novel Lead-Free Double Perovskite \b{eta}2SnGeX6 (\b{eta} = K, Rb; X = Cl, Br, I) for thermomechanical, optoelectronic and outstanding thermoelectric applications
